evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG pins, dmi_jtag, and simulation DTM/JTAG requesters
evidence_slots.operation: issue DMI/debug transactions that can activate/reset/request debug
evidence_slots.object: debug control outputs and debug module state: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Explicit debug authorization/authentication or lifecycle/test-mode guard before DMI/JTAG-originated requests can reach debug-module control outputs.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: SimDTM under RISCV_FESVR_SIM drives debug_req_valid/debug_req. Otherwise dmi_jtag receives testmode_i and hash signals and outputs jtag_unlock plus dmi_req/dmi_req_valid. Physical JTAG pins are assigned directly to tck/tms/trst_n/tdi in the non-simulation path. The visible facts/observations do not show a top-level authorization gate between debug_req/debug_req_valid and the debug module outputs.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG pins tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or SimDTM/SimJTAG -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> debug module/dm_top -> ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/debug_req_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: Non-simulation JTAG path is active in the `else` of RISCV_FESVR_SIM; SimDTM path is active when RISCV_FESVR_SIM is defined. testmode_i is passed into dmi_jtag, but no top-level authorization condition was observed gating DMI requests. Reset/trst affect operation but are not authorization controls.
evidence_slots.impact: Enabled JTAG or simulation DTM can reach debug-module controls, potentially allowing debug entry/reset/activation without a visible authorization decision.

rtl_evidence.text: RISCV_FESVR_SIM SimDTM drives debug_req_valid, debug_req.addr/op/data, and debug_resp handshake.
dmi_jtag instance takes testmode_i, jtag_hash_i, okey_hash_i, ikey_hash_i and outputs jtag_unlock_o, dmi_req_o, dmi_req_valid_o.
physical JTAG pins are assigned to tck/tms/trst_n/tdi and td_o/tdo_oe_o.
top-level debug outputs exist; debug module path consumes DMI signals and drives control outputs per observation.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: JTAG/DTM-originated DMI requests are wired into the debug module path that drives debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, and dmactive_o without a visible top-level authorization/lifecycle gate; simulation DTM can drive the same path when enabled.
inspection_records.notes: Full internals of dmi_jtag/dm_top were not provided; finding is based on visible top-level reachability and absence of a visible explicit guard.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG/debug unlock source via dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o
evidence_slots.object: reglk_mem/reglk_ctrl_o lock bits consumed by protected wrappers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Authenticated debug authorization plus sticky lock/lifecycle policy; JTAG unlock should not clear/bypass sticky lock bits after set except under explicit permitted lifecycle/full-reset condition.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: reglk_wrapper clears all reglk_mem entries whenever ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9) is true. Thus assertion of jtag_unlock clears reglk_mem to 'h0 and drives reglk_ctrl_o unlocked. Top-level connects dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o to reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock. Normal writes are gated by acct_ctrl_i and selected reglk_ctrl bits, but jtag_unlock bypasses those normal write guards through the reset branch.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o -> riscv_peripherals.jtag_unlock -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_mem reset to 0 -> reglk_ctrl_o -> hmac/pkt/acct/reset/etc. reglk_ctrl_i consumers
evidence_slots.state_condition: reglk_mem clears on global reset, rst_9, or jtag_unlock assertion. en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i only gates normal AXI writes/reads, not the jtag_unlock reset branch.
evidence_slots.impact: A JTAG/debug unlock pulse can deassert all lock bits and reopen writes/reads to lock-protected peripheral registers, undermining HMAC, access-control, reset, fuse, and other wrapper protections.

rtl_evidence.text: dmi_jtag outputs jtag_unlock_o to jtag_unlock.
reglk_wrapper connects reglk_ctrl_o to global reglk_ctrl and jtag_unlock to the dmi_jtag-produced signal.
reglk_ctrl_o is concatenation of reglk_mem[5:0].
if ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9), all reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0.
normal writes update lock memory only under en && we and selected lock bits.
HMAC control/data/key-derived writes depend on reglk_ctrl_i bits.
access-control policy writes depend on reglk_ctrl bits.
selected PKT/fuse reads depend on reglk_ctrl_i bits.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: jtag_unlock is used as a reset-like condition in reglk_wrapper that clears all lock memory to zero, allowing JTAG/debug unlock to remove sticky register locks after they are set.
inspection_records.notes: Hash-related inputs to dmi_jtag exist, but the provided evidence does not show that jtag_unlock is authenticated strongly enough or lifecycle-limited before clearing locks.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester through HMAC NoC AXI-lite bridge
evidence_slots.operation: MMIO read/write/configure/start hashing and read HMAC state
evidence_slots.object: HMAC key registers, derived ikey/okey bytes, message data, hash output, and rdata
evidence_slots.expected_guard: HMAC key material and internal/derived state should be protected from unauthorized MMIO reads/writes by privilege/accounting and register locks; reads should not expose secret key or derived pads unless explicitly permitted.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: HMAC wrapper gates all accesses with en=en_acct && acct_ctrl_i and individual read/write cases with reglk_ctrl_i. However, ikey_hash_bytes and okey_hash_bytes are explicitly readable when reglk_ctrl_i[4] is clear; hash words are readable when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear; data words are readable when reglk_ctrl_i[2] is clear. Key0 and derived arrays are writable when reglk_ctrl_i[5] is clear. The observed reset block clears startHash, newMessage, and data[0:15], but key0/ikey_hash_bytes/okey_hash_bytes/key_hash_bypass clearing was not observed in the provided reset excerpt. debug_mode_i zeroes active key and okey_hash assignment but does not prevent MMIO readout of stored derived arrays.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_hmac_noc2_* -> i_hmac_axilite_bridge -> hmac_axi_req -> hmac_wrapper address decode/write/read mux -> rdata -> hmac_axi_resp -> ariane_hmac_buf_noc3_*
evidence_slots.state_condition: HMAC wrapper instantiated with acct_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][4] and reglk_ctrl[4*8+7:4*8]. Access requires en_acct and acct_ctrl_i. Writes occur on en && we; reads occur on en. debug_mode_i affects key/okey_hash assignment, not the read mux.
evidence_slots.impact: An MMIO requester permitted by current acc_ctrl can read secret-derived HMAC pad state and hash output if locks are unset; incomplete reset of key-related state can leave residue; debug mode does not block readout of stored derived arrays.

rtl_evidence.text: hmac_wrapper instantiated with reglk_ctrl[4*8+7:4*8], acct_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][4], debug_mode_i, rst_4.
HMAC NoC AXI-lite bridge connects external HMAC NoC port to hmac_axi_req/resp.
key is zeroed in debug_mode_i, okey_hash is zeroed in debug_mode_i, ikey_hash always built from ikey_hash_bytes.
en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.
visible reset clears startHash, newMessage, and data[0:15].
control/data/key0 writes are gated by reglk_ctrl_i bits.
read mux exposes data, hashValid, and hash words depending on reglk_ctrl_i[2]/[6].
read mux exposes ikey_hash_bytes and okey_hash_bytes when reglk_ctrl_i[4] is clear.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: HMAC MMIO read mux exposes derived key pad/hash state and hash output when corresponding lock bits are clear; key/derived state writes are only lock/accounting gated and key-related arrays appear not fully reset in the visible reset logic.
inspection_records.notes: Finding does not assert unprivileged access when acc_ctrl denies access; issue is insufficient protection/exposure under current policy/lock defaults and possible reset residue.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester through pkt_wrapper
evidence_slots.operation: request/read fuse data and configure fuse address
evidence_slots.object: fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i, pkey_loc, MMIO rdata
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Fuse/key-location access should be mediated by privilege/accounting, register locks, and address/range authorization; fuse read data should not be exposed to unprivileged MMIO reads or attacker-controlled addresses.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pkt_wrapper gates accesses with en=en_acct && acct_ctrl_i, but writes to fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o are not gated by reglk_ctrl_i. The read mux initializes rdata to fuse_rdata_i whenever en is true and explicitly returns fuse_rdata_i at address index 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear. pkey_loc halves are readable when reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] are clear. fuse_addr_o is driven directly from wdata; the only visible range-related behavior is default rdata=0 if fuse_addr_o <= 110, which is not an authorization/range check for issuing fuse requests.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_pkt_noc2_* -> pkt AXI-lite bridge -> pkt_wrapper MMIO decode -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_mem -> fuse_rdata_i -> pkt_wrapper rdata -> ariane_pkt_buf_noc3_*
evidence_slots.state_condition: fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o reset on ~(rst_ni && ~rst_5). On en && we, address[7:3]==0 writes fuse_req_o=wdata[0] and ==1 writes fuse_addr_o=wdata. Reads require en. Top-level connects pkt_wrapper fuse signals to fuse_mem and gates pkt_wrapper acct_ctrl_i with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5].
evidence_slots.impact: A permitted MMIO requester can select arbitrary fuse_addr_o, issue fuse requests, and read fuse_rdata_i/key-location outputs when locks are clear, enabling exposure of fuse/key-location data.

rtl_evidence.text: en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.
on en && we, address 0 writes fuse_req_o and address 1 writes fuse_addr_o from wdata, without reglk_ctrl_i gating.
read mux returns fuse_rdata_i by default under en and at address 4 when reglk_ctrl_i[6] is clear; pkey_loc readable with reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] clear.
pkt submodule consumes fuse_addr_o and produces pkey_loc.
pkt_wrapper acct_ctrl_i tied to acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5].
fuse_mem outputs jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash and returns fuse_rdata for req_i=fuse_req, addr_i=fuse_addr.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: pkt_wrapper allows MMIO-controlled fuse request/address and exposes fuse_rdata_i directly through rdata when enabled; no address range authorization beyond a weak default condition is visible.
inspection_records.notes: Address decode for the pkt bridge was only partially shown, but direct wrapper behavior is sufficient for a candidate finding.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible AXI/MMIO requester to acct_wrapper
evidence_slots.operation: write/configure access-control policy and use policy to permit peripheral requests
evidence_slots.object: acct_mem/acc_ctrl_o policy bits and downstream acc_ctrl_c gates
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Access-control policy updates should be restricted by a lock/privilege guard and not be writable by subjects whose later accesses are controlled by that same policy; all protected request paths should consult policy consistently.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: acct_wrapper exports acc_ctrl_o from acct_mem with acct_mem[0] ORed with {8{we_flag}}. On reset/rst_6, all acct_mem entries are set to 32'hffffffff, a fully permissive value. Normal policy writes require en=en_acct && acct_ctrl_i and are gated by reglk_ctrl bits. Top-level feeds acct_wrapper.acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6], so ability to modify policy is itself determined by current policy. acc_ctrl_c[j] is computed as acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]), causing peripheral 5 to inherit peripheral 4 permission. Search observations show many protected wrappers use acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index], but coverage is not fully proven for debug/reset paths.
evidence_slots.path: acct NoC port -> i_acct_axilite_bridge -> acct_wrapper write decode -> acct_mem/acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] -> wrapper acct_ctrl_i/request gates
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset/rst_6 initializes policy entries to all ones. Writes occur on en && we, with en requiring acct_ctrl_i. acct_wrapper is instantiated with reglk_ctrl[6*8+7:6*8] and acct_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6]. we_flag_0 can force bits in acc_ctrl_o for acct_mem[0].
evidence_slots.impact: Permissive reset defaults and self-modifiable policy can allow a subject with current access to alter future access-control enforcement; special aliasing may unintentionally grant PKT/fuse permissions from HMAC permissions; incomplete coverage could permit bypasses.

rtl_evidence.text: acc_ctrl_o = {acct_mem[2], acct_mem[1], acct_mem[0] | {8{we_flag}}}.
en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i.
reset/rst_6 sets all acct_mem[j] <= 32'hffffffff.
normal writes update acct_mem when selected reglk_ctrl bits are clear.
acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]).
boot ROM request gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0].
acct_wrapper instantiated with acct_ctrl_i=acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6] and acc_ctrl_o=acc_ctrl.
acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i] used for multiple wrappers at indices 12,13,10,1,2,11,3,4,5,6,9,8.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Access-control policy state is self-referentially gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6] and defaults permissive on reset; policy writes are lock-gated but can be modified by currently permitted requesters, and the special acc_ctrl_c alias can create permission inheritance.
inspection_records.notes: Full downstream coverage could not be proven from truncated observations; finding centers on policy update/default/alias behavior.

evidence_slots.subject: external NoC/AXI requester or bus master
evidence_slots.operation: route/read/write MMIO transactions to protected peripherals
evidence_slots.object: protected peripheral MMIO regions including hmac, pkt/fuse, reglk, acct, debug, reset, crypto endpoints
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Each externally reachable ingress should have address-range decode plus privilege/accounting/lock mediation before read/write enables reach protected registers or side-effect outputs; read/write paths should enforce consistently and denied/default responses should be safe.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level exposes separate NoC ports for HMAC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DEBUG, reset, and other crypto/security endpoints. HMAC/PKT/ACCT/REGLK bridges feed AXI-lite wrapper requests, where en is usually gated by acct_ctrl_i. However, the bridge itself has no visible permission check; write/read strobes reach wrapper interfaces and rely on wrapper en logic. The debug NoC path connects through i_debug_axilite_bridge to dm_master/axi2mem/debug module path without an observed acc_ctrl_c gate. PKT fuse writes are not reglk-gated inside the wrapper, and HMAC reads can expose derived state when locks are clear.
evidence_slots.path: NoC ingress ports -> noc_axilite_bridge instances -> wrapper AXI-lite requests/dm_master -> protected wrapper read/write decode or debug module
evidence_slots.state_condition: acc_ctrl_c is used at many wrapper instantiations; HMAC uses index 4, PKT index 5, ACCT index 6, REGLK index 9. Debug bridge observations at lines 414-453 did not show acc_ctrl_c mediation. Address/denied default behavior across all endpoints was not fully available.
evidence_slots.impact: If a NoC requester can reach a protected endpoint, access control relies on per-wrapper policy and may be absent for debug/fabric paths or inconsistent between reads and writes, enabling unauthorized routing or side-effect access.

rtl_evidence.text: top-level ports expose many NoC ingress/egress paths for RSA/RNG/debug/bootrom/clint/plic/AES/reset/SHA/HMAC/PKT/ACCT/REGLK/DMA.
debug noc_axilite_bridge maps debug NoC port to dm_master AXI-lite signals; no acc_ctrl_c gate observed in this excerpt.
HMAC NoC AXI-lite bridge maps external HMAC NoC port to hmac_axi_req/resp.
PKT AXI request tie-offs and bridge path to pkt_axi_req/resp observed.
ACCT NoC AXI-lite bridge maps external ACCT NoC port to acct_axi_req/resp.
REGLK NoC AXI-lite bridge maps external REGLK NoC port to reglk_axi_req/resp.
acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i] used at many wrapper instantiations, but not shown for debug bridge.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Multiple externally reachable NoC-to-AXI-lite bridges route directly to protected wrappers; wrapper-level acc_ctrl gates exist for many endpoints, but the debug NoC path and some side-effect/read paths show no visible acc_ctrl/lock mediation at the fabric bridge itself.
inspection_records.notes: Because address decode/crossbar logic was truncated, endpoint-by-endpoint bypass comparison is incomplete; status remains candidate rather than fully proven global violation.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible MMIO/NoC requester controlling reset path
evidence_slots.operation: trigger peripheral reset or lifecycle-like reset of security wrappers
evidence_slots.object: rst_4/rst_5/rst_6/rst_9 and affected HMAC, PKT/fuse, ACCT policy, REGLK lock state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Software-triggered or bus-triggered resets of security peripherals should require privilege/accounting/lock authorization and should not leave protected state in unsafe defaults or partially reset access controls.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level declares rst_1..rst_13 and passes rst_4 to HMAC, rst_5 to PKT, rst_6 to ACCT, and rst_9 to REGLK. Reset-control generation/update logic was not provided. Wrapper reset effects include: reglk_wrapper clears all reglk_mem to 0 (unlocked) on rst_9; acct_wrapper sets acct_mem to 32'hffffffff (permissive) on rst_6; pkt_wrapper clears fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o on rst_5; HMAC clears control/data on rst_4 but visible reset excerpt does not clear key/derived arrays.
evidence_slots.path: reset-control NoC/MMIO path -> rst_* signals -> hmac_wrapper/pkt_wrapper/acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper reset branches -> protected state defaults
evidence_slots.state_condition: reglk reset condition is ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9). acct reset condition is ~(rst_ni && ~rst_6). pkt reset condition is ~(rst_ni && ~rst_5). hmac reset condition is ~(rst_ni && ~rst_4). Reset-control decode/update and authorization were missing from observations.
evidence_slots.impact: If rst_* are software/NoC controllable without strong authorization, an attacker can clear sticky locks, restore permissive access-control defaults, or create partial resets/residue in secret-bearing HMAC state.

rtl_evidence.text: rst_1..rst_13 signals declared.
hmac_wrapper receives rst_4.
pkt_wrapper receives rst_5 and acct_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][5].
acct_wrapper receives rst_6.
reglk_wrapper receives rst_9.
rst_9 clears all reglk_mem to zero/unlocked.
rst_6/global reset sets all acct_mem to 32'hffffffff.
rst_5/global reset clears fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o.
rst_4/global reset clears control/data in visible block; key-related array clearing not observed.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Software/NoC-controlled reset signals feed security wrappers and reset protected state to permissive or cleared defaults; reset-control guard is not visible in the provided evidence.
inspection_records.notes: Reset-control MMIO decode/update was not included; candidate finding is conditional on software/NoC controllability indicated by obligation/fact layer.

evidence_slots.subject: debug-mode controller or requester
evidence_slots.operation: debug-mode bypass/alteration of HMAC controls or state and potential readout through MMIO
evidence_slots.object: HMAC key, ikey_hash/okey_hash derived state, key_hash_bypass, hash/internal state, rdata
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug mode should not relax HMAC key/state permissions unless debug access is separately authenticated and authorized; if debug mode changes readable data or write guards, it must not expose secret state.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: hmac_wrapper takes debug_mode_i. Assignments set key to zero when debug_mode_i is high and set okey_hash to zero when debug_mode_i is high, while ikey_hash is always built from ikey_hash_bytes. No observed read-mux condition checks debug_mode_i; ikey_hash_bytes and okey_hash_bytes remain readable according to reglk_ctrl_i[4], and hash words according to reglk_ctrl_i[6]. Writes to key0/derived arrays are gated by reglk_ctrl_i[5] and en/acct_ctrl_i, not debug authorization. Top-level passes debug_mode_i directly to hmac_wrapper; source/trust/auth of debug_mode_i is not shown.
evidence_slots.path: top-level debug_mode_i -> hmac_wrapper key/okey_hash assignments and access logic -> HMAC instance/read mux -> rdata/NoC response
evidence_slots.state_condition: HMAC instantiated with debug_mode_i at top-level. debug_mode_i affects key and okey_hash assignments at lines 54 and 56. en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; read mux uses en and reglk_ctrl_i bits, not debug_mode_i, in the observed lines.
evidence_slots.impact: A debug-mode transition can alter HMAC computation semantics without a visible authorization check and does not prevent stored secret-derived arrays from being read over MMIO when locks permit; debug access may therefore enable bypass or state exposure paths.

rtl_evidence.text: top-level has debug_mode_i input.
hmac_wrapper receives debug_mode_i from top-level.
hmac_wrapper ports include reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, debug_mode_i, AXI request/response.
debug_mode_i forces key and okey_hash to zero, but ikey_hash is always built from ikey_hash_bytes.
access enable depends on acct_ctrl_i, not debug_mode_i.
read mux exposes data/hash/ikey_hash_bytes/okey_hash_bytes based on reglk_ctrl_i, with no observed debug_mode_i guard.
HMAC instance consumes key, ikey_hash, okey_hash, key_hash_bypass, message_i, and produces hash.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: debug_mode_i changes active HMAC key/okey_hash behavior but does not mediate or block MMIO readout of stored derived HMAC state; debug-mode source authorization is not visible.
inspection_records.notes: No evidence was provided for the authorization/trust boundary of debug_mode_i; finding is based on observed use sites and lack of debug-specific access checks.
